original_user_request: Create a 3D Crossy Road game in a single HTML file using Three.js from CDN
precise_rewrite: Develop a 3D Crossy Road game that runs entirely within a single HTML file, utilizing the Three.js library hosted via a Content Delivery Network (CDN)
assumptions: The user desires a fully self-contained solution without external dependencies beyond the CDN
Performance and responsiveness are critical for the game's experience

tradeoffs.name: Speed vs Quality
tradeoffs.rationale: Balancing the time required to develop the game against its visual fidelity and gameplay mechanics

tradeoffs.name: Realism vs Fun
tradeoffs.rationale: Deciding whether to prioritize a more realistic 3D environment or a more engaging, simplified gameplay experience

tradeoffs.name: Security vs Cost
tradeoffs.rationale: Evaluating the trade-off between ensuring the game is safe for users and minimizing development costs

speed.rationale: The user wants a quick solution to get started with the game

quality.rationale: While speed is important, maintaining a good level of visual and gameplay quality is crucial

realism.rationale: Realism is less critical than fun and speed, but still an important factor for the game's appeal

fun.rationale: The primary goal of the game is to be enjoyable, so maximizing fun should be a top priority

security.rationale: Security is important but not as critical as speed and fun. The CDN-based approach minimizes security risks

cost.rationale: Cost is the least critical factor, given the simplicity of using a CDN for Three.js

definition_of_done: A single HTML file containing all necessary code and assets
The game should be playable in any modern web browser
The game must use Three.js from a CDN
Basic gameplay mechanics of Crossy Road implemented
non_goals: Incorporating advanced physics or complex AI for vehicles
Creating high-resolution textures or detailed models
conflict_rules: If speed and quality conflict, prioritize speed to ensure the game is quickly available
If realism and fun conflict, prioritize fun to maintain player engagement
If security and cost conflict, prioritize security to protect users from potential risks
